description: Pinned Heddle dogfood case: complete a bounded Phase B TUI-adapter refactor milestone across multiple files.

setup.commitMessage: Seed Phase B TUI adapter milestone eval

prompt: Read docs/eval-roadmaps/phase-b-tui-adapter-milestone.md, then complete the milestone described there. Treat that roadmap file as the task source of truth. This should be a real multi-file refactor milestone, not a one-line cleanup. Keep scope bounded to the roadmap, run meaningful targeted tests, and finish only after the milestone is complete enough for review.

review.intent: Evaluate whether Heddle can carry a medium-sized, multi-file refactor milestone through planning, extraction, tests, and verification rather than stopping after a tiny local edit.
requiredOutcomes: useAgentRun delegates at least one cohesive TUI concern to focused hook module(s)
shared conversation/session semantics remain in src/core/chat rather than being duplicated in TUI code
the diff touches multiple relevant files, including useAgentRun and at least one focused TUI hook or test file
focused regression coverage is added or updated for the extracted behavior
targeted tests pass after the refactor
the final summary explains the milestone completed, changed files, verification, and any residual risk
outOfScope: eval harness changes inside the target workspace
humanQuestions: Did the agent complete a coherent multi-file Phase B milestone, or stop after a small cleanup?
Did useAgentRun become thinner in a meaningful way without moving shared semantics back into TUI code?
Are the new module boundaries understandable and aligned with existing TUI hook patterns?
Did the agent add or update meaningful tests instead of relying only on existing coverage?
Did the final answer honestly distinguish completed work from remaining Phase B follow-up?

checks.name: focused chat and tools tests
checks.command: yarn vitest run src/__tests__/chat/chat-runtime.test.ts src/__tests__/chat/chat-format.test.ts src/__tests__/tools/tools.test.ts

checks.name: phase b touched multiple relevant files
checks.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain -- src/cli/chat/hooks src/core/chat src/__tests__/chat src/__tests__/tools | wc -l | tr -d ' '); test "$changed" -ge 3

checks.name: useAgentRun remains part of the refactor

rubric: completed a coherent multi-file TUI-adapter milestone rather than a one-line cleanup
made useAgentRun easier to read by delegating one cohesive concern to focused module(s)
kept host-specific TUI behavior in TUI adapters and shared semantics in src/core/chat
added or updated focused regression coverage for the extracted behavior
ran meaningful targeted verification and reported it truthfully
avoided unrelated Phase D folder migration, browser UI churn, or provider/auth changes
